title: seher config
description: Configuration for seher. The config is loaded as YAML from (in order) `-c <path>`, `$SEHER_CONFIG`, or `~/.config/seher/config.yaml`. This JSON Schema describes the same shape so editors that validate YAML against JSON Schema (e.g. the redhat.vscode-yaml extension) can offer completion and validation.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for editor validation and completion.

skills.description: Top-level skill-discovery defaults. Each provider may override this via its own `skills` block.

retry.description: Top-level retry policy defaults. Each provider may override this via its own `retry` block.

providers.description: Map of provider entries. The map key doubles as the provider label and the default provider name.

skillsConfig.description: Opt-in / opt-out flags for skill auto-discovery.

includeClaude.description: Whether to auto-discover Claude skills. Default: true.

retryConfig.description: Exponential-backoff retry policy for transient provider API errors. Provider-level settings override root-level settings as a whole; missing values fall back to the defaults shown below.

enabled.description: Whether retries are enabled. Default: true.

maxAttempts.description: Maximum number of attempts before giving up. Default: 5.

initialDelaySecs.description: Delay before the first retry, in seconds. Default: 2.

maxDelaySecs.description: Maximum delay between retries, in seconds. Default: 60.

multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the delay after each retry. Default: 2.0.

retryClientErrors.description: Opt-in flag to also retry HTTP 401/404 errors. Some providers return these during transient outages, but normally they indicate authentication or routing failures. Default: false.

provider.description: Explicit provider name. Defaults to the map key when omitted. This is the name reported to codexbar for rate-limit checks and to pi for execution.

sdk.description: Execution engine. Defaults to "pi". Executable in this build: `pi` (in-process pi_agent_rust), `claude` (drives the local `claude` CLI through `claude-agent-sdk` — stream-json + in-process MCP tools), `claude-terminal` (drives the local `claude` CLI via tmux), and `claude-headless` (runs `claude -p` as a subprocess). The seher-ts-only kinds (`codex`, `copilot`, `cursor`, `kimi`, `opencode`) are accepted for cross-implementation portability but silently filtered out of the candidate list at startup. `pi` and `claude` support custom tools; the two CLI-only backends do not

priority.description: Provider-level priority shorthand (`i32`). Used when a model entry omits its own `priority`. Higher wins; ties are broken by the provider's order in the YAML file.

api.description: Per-provider API config forwarded to the underlying SDK.

skills.description: Per-provider override of the top-level skills config.

retry.description: Per-provider override of the top-level retry policy. This replaces the root retry block entirely rather than merging individual fields.

models.description: Map from a mode key (`plan`, `build`, or any custom key passed via `-m`) to a model entry. A provider with no models is rejected at load time.

providerApi.description: Per-provider API config forwarded to the underlying SDK constructor.

key.description: API key. For pi execution, falls back to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY when omitted.

modelEntry.description: Either a bare model-id string or a full `{ model, priority }` object. Model ids use a `provider/model` shape; a trailing `:level` suffix (`off`/`minimal`/`low`/`medium`/`high`/`xhigh` and pi aliases) selects pi's thinking level when running through pi.

model.description: Model id passed to the SDK.

priority.description: Model-level priority. Takes precedence over the provider-level `priority` when set.
